title: tls ca file | directory
text: tls ca file | directory On Unix, specify the location of the CA certificate used to validate the LDAP server. USYS$LDP_PARAMS { = } tls ca file | tlscafil = PemFIle USYS$LDP_PARAMS { = } tls ca directory | tlscadir = CaDir Example: USYS$LDP_PARAMS tlscafil=/certicates/certificate_file.pem Arguments PemFIle —fully-qualified file name of the CA certificate in PEM format. CaDir —directory containing the CA certificates with hashed file names. Description On Unix, the CA certificate required to validate the LDAP server may be specified as a PEM file or in a directory of hashed file names. The options tls ca file and tls ca directory are mutually exclusive. Setting either one implicitly sets tls server validation =validate (if it is not specified). Related Links LDAP over TLS tls server validation Instruct the LDAP connector to connect to the LDAP server using TLS. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
